arts.ttl: Arsenal told final Dani Olmo decision as January transfer window reality clear
arts.sum: Arsenal have been told that Dani Olmo is not considering a move away from Barcelona, despite the club’s troubles in registering the player. That could potentially free up finances to be able to register Olmo, but it’s now a race against time.

arts.ttl: 10 Arsenal transfers Mikel Arteta could complete as soon as January window opens
arts.sum: From January, players whose contracts expire at the end of the season will be able to negotiate a pre-contract agreement with clubs outside of the country in the league they play in. For Arsenal, this will mean Premier League players are off-limits, so the Gunners won't be able to agree deals for the likes of Mohamed Salah, Kevin De Bruyne, Trent Alexander-Arnold, Son Heung-Min and Virgil van Dijk.

arts.ttl: Arsenal maintain interest in Real Madrid starlet
arts.sum: The brass at Premier League giants Arsenal have maintained their long-standing interest in the signing of Real Madrid starlet Arda Güler. That’s according to transfer insider Sami Mokbel of the…

arts.ttl: Gary Neville reveals why Arsenal still have Premier League title advantage over Liverpool
arts.sum: "Arsenal have been through the mill in the last couple of years Sometimes, when you win your first title, it doesn’t happen in the most expected way, and things can happen." Though Arteta looks to have lost Saka for a number of months, a particular Liverpool injury blow would be worse in the opinion of Neville. As for Liverpool, following a clash against Manchester United, the Reds play Nottingham Forest, Brentford and Ipswich in league action.

arts.ttl: Arsenal predicted lineup vs Brentford - Premier League
arts.sum: Predicting how Arsenal could line up for their New Year's Day Premier League clash against Brentford.

arts.ttl: Why former Chelsea and Arsenal star Willian is now a free agent after failed 120-day spell
arts.sum: Willian is confident he still has what it takes to play at a high level. The Brazilian, who clinched the Premier League title twice during his seven-year spell at Chelsea under Jose Mourinho, has had a rocky journey since departing Stamford Bridge in 2020.

arts.ttl: Three Arsenal stars free to talk to other clubs in January as Mikel Arteta set contract deadline
arts.sum: And now, the Gunners are in a position where they could bolster their ranks, but also risk losing key players who might be tempted by pre-contract agreements with overseas clubs come Wednesday (January 1). The north London club chose not to take up the option to prolong his contract by another year, and Tierney has been informed he can look for a new team.

arts.ttl: Partey decision, Havertz role choice and Calafiori chance - Arsenal line-ups vs Brentford - Sam Truelove - football.london
arts.sum: Canton’s Arsenal XI vs Brentford: Raya; Timber, Saliba, Gabriel, Calafiori; Rice, Merino, Odegaard, Jesus, Havertz, Martinelli Truelove’s Arsenal XI vs Brentford: Raya; Timber, Saliba, Gabriel, Lewis-Skelly; Partey, Rice, Odegaard; Jesus, Havertz, Martinelli

arts.ttl: Arsenal injury boost as Mikel Arteta continues to plan without Bukayo Saka
arts.sum: Ethan Nwaneri was the only offensive option Arteta had to choose from and did not even come on as the Spaniard made just two changes in the game that saw his side narrowly claim the three points. “He was gutted because he had a good game against Palace here, he could have scored two goals and made an assist and in the end, he got injured in a training session,” Arteta said.

arts.ttl: Brentford vs. Arsenal preview: Goals galore in Premier League derby as Mikel Arteta keeps faith in Brazilian
arts.sum: Arsenal's Premier League London derby with Brentford on New Year's Day has "goals written all over it", Gunners expert Charles Watts believes.
